title: Navitas Semiconductor Set to Report Q1 Earnings: What's in Store for the Stock?
text: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) is scheduled to announce its first-quarter 2026 results on May 5. The company anticipated revenues between $8 million and $8.5 million for the first quarter of 2026, with the consensus estimate at $8.1 million—representing a 42.3% year-over-year decline. Analysts forecast a loss of 5 cents per share, unchanged for 60 days. The company reported a 6-cent loss in the comparable prior-year quarter. Notably, Navitas has matched consensus estimates in each of the four trailing quarters. The semiconductor manufacturer benefits from expanding demand for its gallium nitride (GaN) and silicon carbide (SiC) technologies, particularly for AI data center infrastructure. The company expects sequential revenue growth supported by stronger sales traction across high-power markets where these solutions are gaining adoption. Despite opportunities, Navitas faces challenges including significantly reduced revenues due to its exit from low-power mobile markets, insufficient scale to absorb fixed costs, and early-stage customer adoption limiting immediate revenue acceleration. Zacks assigned the stock a Rank #4 (Sell) with 0.00% Earnings Surprise Probability, suggesting limited likelihood of beating expectations.
